subject: Project Management
title: Design Phase
description: A quick win: understand Design Phase.

hook.text: The design phase translates requirements into architecture: how the system will be structured and built.

buildup.text: Architects create detailed blueprints: system diagrams, database schemas, interface designs, and technical specifications.

discovery.text: The output is often a 'Big Design Up Front' — BDUF — a comprehensive plan covering every detail before coding.

twist.text: BDUF assumes you can foresee every technical challenge. In practice, the hardest problems only emerge during coding.

climax.text: Design phase documents become shelfware — detailed plans nobody reads once coding reveals the real complexity.

punchline.text: No design survives contact with actual code.

quiz.question: What does BDUF stand for?
options: Big Design Up Front — a comprehensive plan created before coding
